title: Science Terms
description: Key biology, chemistry, and physics vocabulary

cards.back: The basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms
Cell
The organelle that contains DNA and controls cell activities
Nucleus
Organelle that produces energy (ATP) — the 'powerhouse of the cell'
Mitochondria
Organelle in plant cells where photosynthesis occurs
Chloroplast
Process by which plants convert sunlight, water, and CO₂ into glucose and oxygen
Photosynthesis
Process that breaks down glucose to produce ATP (energy) using oxygen
Cell division producing two identical daughter cells
Mitosis
Cell division producing four genetically unique sex cells (gametes)
Meiosis
Deoxyribonucleic acid — molecule carrying genetic instructions
DNA
Ribonucleic acid — helps translate DNA into proteins
A segment of DNA that codes for a specific protein or trait
A coiled structure of DNA and proteins found in the nucleus

cards.front: How many chromosomes do humans have?
cards.back: 46 (23 pairs)
A trait that is expressed when at least one dominant allele is present
A trait that is only expressed when two recessive alleles are present
The genetic makeup of an organism (e.g. Bb)
The observable physical characteristics of an organism
Change in a population's inherited traits over generations
Process where organisms with favorable traits survive and reproduce more
The science of classifying organisms

cards.front: Order of taxonomic classification (broadest to narrowest)
cards.back: Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
A community of living organisms and their physical environment
A sequence showing how energy transfers from one organism to another
An organism that makes its own food (e.g. plants)
An organism that eats other organisms for energy
An organism that breaks down dead matter (e.g. fungi, bacteria)
The smallest unit of an element that retains its chemical properties
Atom
Positively charged particle in the nucleus of an atom
Neutral (no charge) particle in the nucleus of an atom
Negatively charged particle orbiting the nucleus of an atom
The number of protons in an atom's nucleus — defines the element

cards.front: What defines which element an atom is?
cards.back: Its atomic number (number of protons)
An atom with a net electric charge due to gaining or losing electrons
Atoms of the same element with different numbers of neutrons
Two or more atoms bonded together
A substance made of two or more different elements chemically bonded
A chemical bond formed by sharing electrons between atoms
Covalent bond
A chemical bond formed by transferring electrons from one atom to another
Ionic bond
Measures acidity/alkalinity from 0 (most acidic) to 14 (most basic); 7 is neutral
Neutral (neither acid nor base)
A substance with pH below 7 that donates hydrogen ions (H⁺)
A substance with pH above 7 that accepts hydrogen ions
A process in which substances (reactants) are transformed into new substances (products)
A substance that speeds up a chemical reaction without being consumed
A biological catalyst (protein) that speeds up reactions in living things
Solid, liquid, gas, and plasma
Speed in a given direction (a vector quantity)
Velocity
The rate of change of velocity over time
A push or pull on an object (measured in Newtons)
An object at rest stays at rest, and a moving object stays in motion, unless acted on by a force (inertia)
Newton's First Law
Force equals mass times acceleration (F = ma)
Newton's Second Law of Motion
For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction
The force of attraction between objects with mass
A force that opposes motion between two surfaces in contact
The ability to do work; exists in forms like kinetic, potential, thermal, etc.
Energy of motion
Kinetic energy
Stored energy due to position or condition
Potential energy
Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed
The distance between successive crests of a wave
The number of wave cycles per second (measured in Hertz)
Radio, microwave, infrared, visible light, ultraviolet, X-ray, gamma ray
Approximately 300,000 km/s (3 × 10⁸ m/s)
Mass divided by volume (D = m/v)
Density formula
